rulings.Case Title: SUSAN WHITEHOUSE VS. PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton To Compel Physical And Mental Examinations
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 11, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S Moiton To Compel Physical And Mental Examinations. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL PHYSICAL AND MENTAL EXAMINATIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF, THROUGH HER COMPLAINT AND DISCOVERY RESPONSES HAS CLEARLY PLACED HER PHYSICAL INJURY AND EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AT ISSUE AND DEFENDANT HAS MET THE REQUIREMENTS UNDER CCP SECTION 2032.320. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES C. BOILLOD VS. CHRISTINA M. WENDEL PHD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 11, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANT WILLIAM QUICK DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION (BREACH OF CONTRACT) IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THERE IS NO PROMISED PARTICULARIZED RESULT PLED. DEPENBROK V. KAISER (1978) 79 CAL.APP.3RD 167, 170. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION AND GROUNDS. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES C. BOILLOD VS. CHRISTINA M. WENDEL PHD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 11, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANT WILLIAM QUICK MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO THE PRAYER FOR FEES AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO OBTAIN THE REQUIRED ORDER TO SEEK THOSE DAMAGES. CENTRAL PATHOLOGY SERVICE MEDICAL CLINIC, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1992) 3 CAL.4TH 181. THE MOTION IS DENIED ON THE REMAINING GROUNDS. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: 300 PROSPECT PROPERTIES, INC. VS. NORTH CAPITAL CORPORATION DBA GRACIES CAFE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 11, 2011, line 7, PLAINTIFF 300 PROSPECT PROPERTIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE OPTION ON THE LEASE EXPIRED BY THE TERMS OF THE LEASE AND THERE ARE NO MATERIAL FACTS IN DISPUTE. DEFENDANTS PLED DEFENSES ARE NOT RELEVANT OR SUPPORTED BY THE EVIDENCE. FURTHER, DEFENDANTS ATTEMPT TO ARGUE "WAIVER" AS A DEFENSE IS NOT APPROPRIATELY BEFORE THE COURT IN THAT DEFENDANTS FAILED TO PLEAD THAT IN THEIR ANSWER. =(302/LMG)
